sourceMovie.title: Blonde
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Blonde (2022) stuck because it commits to its drama premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Andrew Dominik's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: Nocturnal Animals
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Blonde clicked for you, Nocturnal Animals (2016) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Blonde clicked for you, Girl, Interrupted (1999) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Blonde?
faq.answer: Try Nocturnal Animals, Girl, Interrupted, Revolutionary Road—each shares DNA with Blonde while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Blonde?
faq.answer: Blonde (2022) is best described as Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Blonde worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 5.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
